name: Stanza (Stanford NLP Toolkit)

performance.notes: Neural models; GPU significantly faster; batch processing recommended for throughput

agent_readiness.notes: Stanford NLP's official Python toolkit. 70+ languages. Pipeline: tokenize → MWT → POS → lemma → NER → depparse → sentiment → coreference. Neural models (biLSTM + transformer) for each task. Stanford CoreNLP integration via server mode. Consistent output format (Document → Sentence → Token). SentimentModel for sentiment (SST-3). NER for 18+ entity types. High accuracy on many benchmarks. Multilingual NLP research standard.

gotchas: Slower than spaCy for simple tasks (neural pipeline overhead)
CPU inference is slow for complex pipelines
Dependency parse tree navigation requires learning Stanza's data model
summary: Stanza is Stanford NLP's Python toolkit supporting 70+ languages with neural models for tokenization, POS, NER, dependency parsing, lemmatization, sentiment, and coreference. High accuracy on NLP benchmarks. Stanford CoreNLP server integration. Consistent document/sentence/token API. Best for high-accuracy multilingual NLP and linguistic research — spaCy is faster for production use cases.
